desc:
You are travelling along the East Road. It stretches to the east and the west
here. To the south, you notice a small orchard. It looks like a good place to
rest your weary feet.
name: The East Road

desc:
You are travelling along the East Road. It stretches to the east and the west
here. To the south, you notice a small orchard. It looks like a good place to
rest your weary feet.
name: The East Road

desc:
You travel onward on the East Road. To the north, you see a huge tree stretching
its branches high into the sky. It looks like it might be fun to climb on it.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road bends to the north and west here. You can also enter the plains to the
east and south.
name: Bend in the East Road

desc:
You travel along the East Road. Here the road bends from the east to the south.
To the north, there is a low hill covered with some fields.
name: Bend in the East Road

desc:
You are travelling onward along the East Road. Looking south, you see a great
expanse of plains stretching out to the horizon.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road runs east and west here. It is bordered on the south by a vast expanse
of plains. The low hill to the north you realise is a hobbit hole. You see the
front porch north of you.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road here bends from the north to the west. To the south, the plains stretch
off into the distance. You see a low hill to the north-west.
name: Bend in the East Road

desc:
Here the East Road bends from east to south around a low hill. To the north is a
small pond. You can see the plain stretching off to the south- east.
name: Bend in the East Road

desc:
You are travelling through plains along the East Road. Looking to either side,
you see that you can leave the road and wander off into the plains.
name: The East Road

desc:
You are travelling through plains along the East Road. Looking to either side,
you see that you can leave the road to the north and wander off into the plains
there. While it is blocked to the south by a dense brush.
name: The East Road

desc:
Tree-covered hills lie to your southeast, Tuckborough and Tookbank nestled among
them. Grassy plains lie to your north, and Michel Delving is somewhere to your
west. The road continues to the east and west.
name: The East Road

desc:
Tree-covered hills lie to your southeast, Tuckborough and Tookbank nestled among
them. Grassy plains lie to your north, and Michel Delving is somewhere to your
west. The road continues to the east and west.
name: The East Road

desc:
Tree-covered hills lie to your southeast, Tuckborough and Tookbank nestled among
them. Grassy plains lie to your north, and Michel Delving is somewhere to your
west. The road continues to the north and west. A path leaves the road, heading
south. A sign is here.
name: The East Road

desc:
Tree-covered hills lie to your southeast, Tuckborough and Tookbank nestled among
them. Fields lie to your north, and Michel Delving is somewhere to your west.
The road continues south and east of you.
name: The East Road

desc:
Tree-covered hills lie to your southeast, Tuckborough and Tookbank nestled among
them. Grassy plains lie to your north, and Michel Delving is somewhere to your
west. The road continues to the north and west.
name: The East Road

desc:
Here, in the meeting of the roads, a small village resides. An occasional hobbit
strolls by, thinking of dinner most likely. To the west is the guard post that
helps protect the village. A small unkept road leads north.
name: Waymeet

desc:
The road continues west as always, leading to the small village of Waymeet. To
the east the road bends towards the heart of the Shire.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road continues ever on westward. The surrounding lands are quiet and serene.
Occasionally, the sounds of hobbit children playing break the calm silence.
name: The East Road

desc:
You are standing on the plains boarding the East Road. To the east you see the
plains starting to become overgrown as you leave the cultivated areas. To the
south is the East Road wandering mindlessly along its merry way.
name: Plains

desc:
Squeezing your way down through the hole, you come out in a small chamber below,
inside the tree. This must be where the squirrels are hiding their nuts for the
cold winter months.
name: Below the Tree

desc:
You stand at the base of a large tree. You can definitely climb it. Looking at
it, you see plenty of easy hand holds and places to put your feet. Glancing
down, you see a small hole going down.
name: At the Base of a Large Tree

desc:
This is one end of this homes' main hall. Against the western wall is a closet
and looking down the hall toward the north you can see many guest rooms and
rooms belonging to the owner's children.
name: In a Hobbit Hole

desc:
You continue along the main hall. To the south is a simple hobbit study, to the
north lies the pantry and the cellar. There are tapestries on the walls here.
name: In a Hobbit Hole

desc:
This is the main hall. To the north, you see the kitchen and the drawing room.
To the south lies the dining room and the parlour.
name: In a Hobbit Hole

desc:
You are standing in the front hall of a hobbit hole. There are many pegs along
the wall to hang cloaks and hoods to dry. There is a rug on the floor to dry
boots or feet.
name: Front Hall

desc:
You are walking between rows and rows of pipeweed. They are neatly laid out and
appear to be well cared for. The field is surrounded by what looks like a sturdy
fence.
name: Pipeweed Field

desc:
You are walking between rows and rows of pipeweed. They are neatly laid out and
appear to be well cared for. The field is surrounded by what looks like a sturdy
fence.
name: Pipeweed Field

desc:
Standing on the edge of the pipeweed fields you see that it stretches west as
far as you can see. These hobbits are definitely serious about their pipeweed.
name: Pipeweed Field

desc:
You are in a small garden. There are rows and rows of various types of
vegetables. A fairly good sized portion of the garden is dedicated to mushrooms.
It looks like these hobbits are able to grow enough here to keep their families
well fed.
name: Garden

desc:
Here a small spring bubbles up from below and flows off to the east. Right here
is it shallow enough to wade in during hot days. It looks cool and refreshing,
inviting you to take a drink.
name: Spring

desc:
You see a small pond. To the west you hear the sound of a small spring bubbling
up from the ground. To the east lies a grassy plain, and south of you is a bend
in the Old East Road.
name: A Small Pond

desc:
You are standing in a field of chest-deep swaying grass. Looking about, you
notice the ground sloping slightly downward and the grass thinning a bit to the
west. To the south, you can see the East Road.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing in a field of chest-deep swaying grass. To the south, you see
the Old East Road winding lazily through this sea of grass.
name: Plains

desc:
All around you are hundreds of quacking ducks. You look around and are blinded
by a flurry of wings. You try to speak, but you cannot even hear your own shouts
over the quacking of the ducks.
name: A Duck Pond

desc:
You are on a bird pond. As you sit here, you watch several ducks swim past as
they quack merrily along their way.
name: A Bird Pond

desc:
Here a small spring gently bubbles up from the ground. The water looks cool and
refreshing. Perhaps almost too good to be true.
name: A Small Spring

desc:
You look about in wonder. This plain appears to be well-tended. There are
various signs of cultivation. You realise that it is allowed to run wild, but in
a controlled state. The plain is teeming with life. Looking about, you see that
you can go north, south, or west. High grass blocks your way to the east. To the
north-west, you can hear the sound of water.
name: A Well-tended Plain

desc:
You look about in wonder. This plain appears to be well-tended. There are
various signs of cultivation. You realise that it is allowed to run wild, but in
a controlled state. The plain is teeming with life. This is the southeast corner
of the plain. You can see that it extends to the north and to the west.
name: A Well-tended Plain

desc:
You are standing in a very deep grass. It appears that no one has come through
here in a very long time. Looking around you think you can see some light
breaking through the grass from the north. All other directions appear to be
impassable.
name: In Very Deep Grass

desc:
You look about in wonder. This plain appears to be well-tended. There are
various signs of cultivation. You realise that is is allowed to run wild, but in
a controlled state. The plain is teeming with life. The plain stretches to the
east and to the south. To the west, the high grass appears to be totally
impassable. Just to the north of here lies a small spring.
name: A Well-tended Plain

desc:
You stand in a simple workshop. One wall is a huge set of book shelves packed
with books on all sorts of topics. Various herbs are hanging about the room to
dry. In the centre of the room stands a huge table cluttered with all sorts of
vials and beakers and such.
name: The Workshop

desc:
You find yourself inside a small and cosy hobbit hole. It appears to be well
tended, but also has the slightly untidy look of a place well-used. The floor is
worn in places, but clean, and the small windows in the flat southern wall are
perfectly clean.
name: Inside a Small Hobbit Hole

desc:
You look about in wonder. This plain appears to be well-tended. There are
various signs of cultivation. You realise that it is allowed to run wild, but in
a controlled state. The plain is teeming with life. The plain continues south
from here. To the west, you see a small spring. North of here is a small hobbit
hole.
name: A Well-tended Plain

desc:
You start to walk down a dusty path through the plains. The plains to the west
look completely impassable. To the east you can enter the plains. Looking to
either side, you occasionally catch a glimpse of movement out of the corner of
your eyes. But you can never get a clear view of what it is.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You start to walk down a dusty path through the plains. The plains to the west
look completely impassable. To the east you can enter the plains. Looking to
either side, you occasionally catch a glimpse of movement out of the corner of
your eyes. But you can never get a clear view of what it is.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You continue to walk down a dusty path through the plains. The plains to the
west look completely impassable. To the east you can enter the plains. Looking
to either side, you occasionally catch a glimpse of movement out of the corner
of your eyes. But you can never get a clear view of what it is.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You are standing waist deep in grass. Looking to the west you see a dusty path
winding its way through these endless plains.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing waist deep in grass. Looking to the east, you see a seemingly
endless sea of grass streatching out in front of you. You consider exploring it
further, but hesitate, worried about that you may become lost. Looking to the
west you see a dusty path winding its way through these endless plains.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing waist deep in grass. Looking to the east, you see a seemingly
endless sea of grass streatching out in front of you. You consider exploring it
further, but hesitate, worried about that you may become lost. Looking to the
west you see a dusty path winding its way through these endless plains.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing waist deep in grass. Looking to the east you see a dusty path
winding its way through these endless plains.
name: Plains

desc:
You start to walk down a dusty path through the plains. The plains to the west
look completely impassable. To the east you can enter the plains. Looking to
either side, you occasionally catch a glimpse of movement out of the corner of
your eyes. But you can never get a clear view of what it is.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You are standing waist deep in grass. Looking to the east, you see a seemingly
endless sea of grass stretching out in front of you. You consider exploring it
further, but hesitate, worried about that you may become lost. Looking to the
west you see a dusty path winding its way through these endless plains.
name: Plains

desc:
The path runs north to south and is bordered on both sides by the plains. To the
west they are completely impassable, but the area to the east seems easier to
travel. Unseen eyes seem to watch your progress here, but it is perhaps just a
trick of the light, or maybe only the small creatures, who scurry about in the
high grasses.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You are standing waist deep in a sea of grass. The plains seem to stretch on
forever to the east and to the south. To the north, you catch sight of the Old
East Road. To the west is a dusty path winding its way south from the Old East
Road. You wonder where it leads.
name: Plains

desc:
You find yourself at the edge of a grassy plain. Though the grass here is only
ankle-deep, it seems to grow thicker and deeper further to the south, and it
seems that it would be easy to get lost in the tall grasses. To the north is the
familiar sight of the East Road, winding lazily across the land.
name: Plains

desc:
You find yourself at the edge of a vast grassy plain. Here the grass comes only
to your ankles, but it seems that it grows longer to the south. To the north the
East Road can be seen, wandering lazily across the countryside.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand on the edge of a grassy plain. Here the grass only comes to your
waist, but it looks like it gets deeper further south. You think about venturing
further south, but fear that you might soon become lost in the high grass.
Looking north, you see the comforting sight of the East Road winding lazily
along.
name: Plains

desc:
You have found the hidden treasure room of the children who built this mighty
fortress. Scattered about is their treasure...
name: The Children's Treasure Room

desc:
As you look around, the crudeness of this hole strikes you with a strange
feeling. It is too clean and well concealed for an animal, yet it is not what
you expect to see for a bandit hide out. There is a low table against one wall.
Light shines through a crude window. There are several sticks lined up against
one wall. Then you realise, this is a fortress for children. It is where they
recreate battles of old. You look around again to remember of your youth.
name: A Hideout

desc:
You stand amid an endless plain of grass. But, here is a small clearing with a
small hill in the centre. The footsteps of many little feet appear to have
trampled down the grass in this area. You hear the rustling of grass behind you
and spinning, but nothing is there. The sound of laughter seems to drift upon
the breeze.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand amid a sea of chest-deep grass. The wind creates waves and caps as it
whistles passing you. Glancing down, you cannot see your feet. Gazing about, you
realise that you have lost the sight of the road.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand amid a sea of chest-deep grass. The wind creates waves and caps as it
whistles passing you. Glancing down, you cannot see your feet. Gazing about, you
realise that you have lost the sight of the road.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand amid a sea of chest-deep grass. The wind creates waves and caps as it
whistles passing you. Glancing down, you cannot see your feet. Gazing about, you
realise that you have lost the sight of the road.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand amid a sea of chest-deep grass. The wind creates waves and caps as it
whistles passing you. Glancing down, you cannot see your feet. Gazing about, you
realise that you have lost the sight of the road.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand amid a sea of chest-deep grass. The wind creates waves and caps as it
whistles passing you. Glancing down, you cannot see your feet. Gazing about, you
realise that you have lost the sight of the road.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand amid a sea of chest-deep grass. The wind creates waves and caps as it
whistles passing you. Glancing down, you cannot see your feet. Gazing about, you
realise that you have lost the sight of the road.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand amid a sea of chest-deep grass. The wind creates waves and caps as it
whistles passing you. Glancing down, you cannot see your feet. Gazing about, you
realise that you have lost the sight of the road.
name: Plains

desc:
You stand amid a sea of chest-deep grass. The wind creates waves and caps as it
whistles passing you. Glancing down, you cannot see your feet. Gazing about, you
realise that you have lost sight of the road.
name: Plains

desc:
It was a bit of a tight fit getting down here, but you seemed to have managed
with only a minimal loss of skin. You look around and say to yourself, "Yep,
this is a foxhole." Upon closer examination, you notice the tracks leading from
one corner of the den to the exit and back.
name: Foxhole

desc:
You are standing in a very narrow east-west corridor. To the east, it slopes
downward. West of you, you notice it extends off beyond the range of your
vision.
name: A Very Narrow Passage

desc:
Dark and narrow, this passage slopes downward at a treacherous angle to the east
of here. Torches hang in small brackets along both walls, but all have long ago
guttered out, leaving smoky stains on the walls above them, but no longer
illuminating the tunnel.
name: A Passage Sloping Down

desc:
You are in a corridor running east and west. It extends both ways off into the
distance. You begin to wonder where you will come out.
name: A Really Long Corridor

desc:
You are standing in a small orchard. The various fruit trees spread their
branches over you, shading you from the sun and the rain. You wander for a bit
enjoying the cool solitude between the trees.
name: A Small Orchard

desc:
You are walking along a dusty path through the fields. Every so often you catch
a glimpse of something in the plains to the west. But, each time you look, it is
gone. You have the feeling that someone or something is watching you from the
edge of the road.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You are walking along a dusty path through the fields. Every so often you catch
a glimpse of something in the plains to the west. But, each time you look, it is
gone. You have the feeling that someone or something is watching you from the
edge of the road.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You walk upon a large plain, mostly covered with grass. Off to the northeast,
you see hills, not far away.
name: Grasslands

desc:
A few sparse trees are scattered across the plains. Forest covers many small
hills, just north of you. The grasslands continue to the west, but heading east,
you would soon find yourself among hills.
name: Grasslands

desc:
A few sparse trees are scattered across the plains. Forest covers many small
hills, just north of you. The grasslands continue to the west, but heading east,
you would soon find yourself among hills.
name: Grasslands

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the east, the forest gets much thicker.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
Though the hills are small, you find travel fairly difficult. Here and there you
see brush, growing wild. Most of the foliage is grass, however. Paths lead
through the hills, going to Tookbank, just northeast of here.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
Tookbank lies just east of you, its little homes deep within the hills. Here and
there, you see trees. You may follow the path north.
name: Path to Tookbank

desc:
The path continues on its way through the hills. You start to see signs of the
forest, east of you. The path goes west and south.
name: Path to Tookbank

desc:
Though the hills are small, you find travel fairly difficult. Here and there you
see brush, growing wild. Here, the forest beings to creep over the land, like a
slow-moving glacier.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the south and east, the forest gets much
thicker.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
This well-travelled path slowly weaves its way among many low hills. Grass
borders the trail, and wild flowers bloom all around you. The Old East Road is
directly north of you, and the path continues to the south.
name: Path to Tookbank

desc:
Though the hills are small, you find travel fairly difficult. Here and there you
see brush, growing wild. However, you are surrounded (mainly) by grass. Looking
around, you see the Old East Road, stretching east and west, just north of you.
A path, east of you, branches off, and the hills die down to the west.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
You have climbed a gentle slope, to stand upon a small hill. West of you, the
terrain falls. The East Road is just north of you, and other, somewhat larger
hills are to your east.
name: Small Hill

desc:
You are on the edge of a grassy plain. Here the grass only comes to your waist,
but it looks like it gets deeper further south. You think about venturing
further south, but fear that you might soon become lost in the high grass.
Looking north, you see the comforting sight of the Old East Road winding lazily
along.
name: Plains

desc:
This gentle slope is covered with grass. Travel is easy, considering you are
off-road. From here, you may walk down to a path, either south or east of you.
name: Gentle Slope

desc:
The path bends here, going south, to lead east, away from the hills, or east,
directly into the range. The path is bordered by flowers, growing amidst a vast
carpet of grass.
name: Path to Tookbank

desc:
You stand upon a path, which (evidently) will take you to Tookbank. To the
north, the path continues, fading from sight, as it enters a small range of
hills.
name: Path to Tookbank

desc:
A few sparse trees are scattered across these plains. Forest covers many small
hills, east of you. There appears to be some sort of trail to the north.
name: Grasslands

desc:
Here the plains are more barren, being entirely grasslands. To the northeast,
hills rise up, providing good homes for hobbits. The grasslands continue to the
south and west.
name: Grasslands

desc:
These hills are somewhat smaller than those to the northeast, fading away
quickly, to the southwest.
name: Low Hills

desc:
This well-travelled path slowly weaves its way among many low hills. Grass
borders the trail, and wild flowers bloom all around you. The path heads north
and west, here.
name: Path to Tookbank

desc:
This well-travelled path slowly weaves its way among many low hills. Grass
borders the trail, and wild flowers bloom all around you. The path heads south
and east.
name: Path to Tookbank

desc:
This well-travelled path slowly weaves its way among many low hills. Grass
borders the trail, and wild flowers bloom all around you. The path makes a T,
heading north, east, and west.
name: Path to Tookbank

desc:
Though the hills are small, you find travel fairly difficult. Here and there you
see brush, growing wild. Most of the foliage is grass, however. Paths lead
through the hills, leading east to Tookbank.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
Standing on the highest hill in the area, this windmill slowly rotates its arms.
It stands tall for a building made by hobbits, squeaking as the old, wooden
wheels turn inside. A ladder leads up, inside.
name: Outside the Old Windmill

desc:
These hills are somewhat smaller than those to the northeast, fading away
quickly, to the southwest.
name: Low Hills

desc:
The squeaky sound is loud in here. Obviously old, it seems though this mill is
no longer in use. You get the feeling it could fall apart any minute ...
name: Inside the Windmill

desc:
Though the hills are small, you find travel fairly difficult. Here and there you
see brush, growing wild. Here, the forest begins to creep over the land, like a
slow-moving glacier.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
You have arrived at Tookbank, a village located in the middle of Tookland. For a
halfling village, there are many regular houses rather than the traditional
halfling homes, the smials. A few hobbits stop to see what you are doing.
name: Tookbank

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the south and east, the forest gets much
thicker.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You are walking among a range of low hills. The hills south of you are larger,
and most are covered with trees. The East Road is just west of you.
name: Low Hills

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the south and east, the forest gets much
thicker. A road lies to the north of you.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the south and east, the forest gets much
thicker. A road lies to the north of you.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
The road bends west-north here. Low grassy hills rise to the south of the road.
Fields and pastures stretch out to the north. At some distance to the west you
can see a small village.
name: The East Road

desc:
You are on a small hobbit farm. Many different types of vegetables grow around
you - potatoes, tomatoes, radish, squash and peppers, to name just a few. A
small farmhouse can be seen in the far southeast corner of the fields. To the
east you can make out a large pasture. To the west and to the north of you the
Old East Road winds through the fields.
name: A Small Farm

desc:
This peaceful road continues ever on westward. The surrounding lands are quiet
and serene. Occasionally the sounds of hobbit children playing in the fields
break the calm silence.
name: The East Road

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You are walking along a trail, winding its way around some forest-covered hills.
Although not very wide, it seem to be frequently used. There are signs of life
everywhere. The trail goes east and west.
name: Small Trail among the Hills

desc:
This hill is covered with trees, as are all of the hills in this area. What
makes this hill stand out is the bare rock face along its side, in which there
seems to be a cave.
name: On a Hill

desc:
You appear to be in a bear's den. Surprised to see something like this in the
Shire, you look around. It all looks rather peaceful. This bear must like
berries.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You are walking along a trail, winding its way around some forest-covered hills.
Although not very wide, it seem to be frequently used. There are signs of life
everywhere. The trail goes east and north.
name: Small Trail among the Hills

desc:
You are walking along a trail, winding its way around some forest-covered hills.
Although not very wide, it seems to be frequently used. There are signs of life
everywhere. The trail leads south and west, where it leads into the village of
Tookbank.
name: Small Trail among the Hills

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
The shelves of this small store are packed with various items, most of which no
respectable hobbit would ever need. However, you are in Tookland. Some Tooks
even leave the Shire! A small sign rests on the counter.
name: The Weapons and Armour Shop

desc:
This modest building houses the Shirriffs of Waymeet. The entrance is in proper
order, and so is a nearby desk. However, next to the desk rests a wooden table
with what appears to be an unfinished meal and a several personal belongings. At
the far end of the room, an empty jail cell sits in silent meditation.
name: Guards Post

desc:
A few sparse trees are scattered across these plains. Forest covers many small
hills, south of you. You may reach the road by walking south or east.
name: Grasslands

desc:
You are on a small north-south path heading from the border road to some fields
to the south. There are many cart tracks running back and forth along this path,
making it seem well used. Green grass and some small trees make up the border of
the road here.
name: A Small Path

desc:
The road bends to the north, or can be followed east towards Waymeet. The fields
here are overgrown with weeds, and the road looks like it doesn't get travelled
very often.
name: The Border Road

desc:
The purpose of this road seems forgotten, as it ends suddenly. The road is in
obvious disrepair, and the fields that lie around it look as if they've been
more cared for than the road itself. To the south of you the road bends east,
continuing on its journey towards Waymeet.
name: The Border Road

desc:
The fields around you must hold enough vegetables to supply the Arthedain army!
Acres and acres of land around you, all covered in various vegetables. You can
see a couple of hobbits tending to the crops in the far corner of the field. To
the east, you can see a vegetable garden.
name: A Corner Field

desc:
Unharvested wheat is all the eye can see for many, many acres. It looks as
though the wheat is ready for harvesting, yet no one has had the time or
resources to finish the task. Tilled fields lie to the north and to the east.
The Old East Road is to the south of you, running to Waymeet in the west.
name: A Field of Hay

desc:
Coming from Waymeet, the road bends to the west. Around, you see the common
fields of all the Shire. It appears that this road is not used very often.
name: The Border Road

desc:
The fields around the road are cultivated, but the road itself seems forgotten.
It looks as if it wasn't used very often and you wonder where it leads. The road
continues west, or you can follow it back eastwards towards Waymeet.
name: The Border Road

desc:
The fields here are covered in rows upon rows of lettuce. For miles, all that
you can see are rows of perfect green heads of lettuce. The hobbit who farms
these lands must really like his lettuce. To the south you can see the East Road
as it continues on its way. Another road cuts through the fields to the north.
name: Cultivated Farmland

desc:
The fields around you are completely covered with carrots, lettuce, tomato
plants, radishes and various other vegetables. And in the middle of it all you
can see a small creek running west-north towards a small pond just to the north.
The hobbit who owns these lands must be truly proud of his fields. At some
distance to the south you can see the Border Road cutting through the farmland.
name: Cultivated Fields

desc:
You are just north of the Border Road which leads from Waymeet to nowhere. Rye,
barley and hops grow on the fields around the road. To the west you can see a
small creek making its way through some more fields.
name: Fields North of the Road

desc:
Many types of vegetables are growing here, although it looks like the main
vegetable is potatoes, as you can see a rather large planting of them in the
south-west corner of the fields. To the east, there is a small pasture, every
other direction yields more fields.
name: A Vegetable Garden

desc:
Before you are a few acres of fields. Many different types of plants and
vegetation grow here in patches of different colours. The fields continue to the
north and to the west; to the south and to the east you can see the Old East
Road.
name: A Small Field

desc:
Many acres of pasture lie before you. Lush green grass grows here, and there are
many cows and horses around. Small gates and fences section the pasture into
smaller areas which are used to hold the individual types of livestock. To the
west you can see a large vegetable garden.
name: A Large Pasture

desc:
The soil here seems perfect for growing crops, and some hobbit farmer is already
taking advantage of this. All around you grow rye, barley, hops, oats, flax and
wheat. All seem ready for harvest. It also looks as though the farmer has taken
the time to install a simple irrigation system in the area to keep his crops wet
and well nourished. The fields continue to the east, and there is a small
orchard to the south.
name: Rich Farmland

desc:
Trees dot the countryside here, as a small orchard grows here. Apple trees seem
to be the main production of this orchard, with some smaller berry bushes and
some pear trees to the side. A small planting of parsnips and barley can also be
found here.
name: Fields and Orchards

desc:
Lush pastures and low rolling hills dot the countryside here. Several small
animals, and some livestock has found a home here. The hills make great hiding
spots for rabbits, and the pastures are great for the cows and horses. To the
west, you can see a small farm.
name: Pasture-Land

desc:
The lands around you are almost completely empty. Every now and then you can see
a small animal run into or out of its hole and scurry off into the distance or
down another hole. The ruins of a small house also lies here, almost buried
under the long grass. You can see a small orchard to the north, and a small farm
to the distant west.
name: Countryside

desc:
You're not really sure where the forest gives way to Tuckborough, but you are
now standing in this old halfling village. Many of the nearby hills have
burrows, or smials as the hobbits call them, but it seems regular houses and
shelters are becoming more and more popular. Some hobbits stop to see what
you're doing.
name: Tuckborough

desc:
This is a a small well-tended barn which contains a big stack of hay as well as
a paddock. This would obviously be a good place to leave your horse.
name: Tuckborough Barn

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
The hills are mostly regular, and the forest is dense. You are glad to be
walking on a trail. You can find your way to civilization, now. The trail goes
north and south.
name: Hunting Trail

desc:
The hills are mostly regular, and the forest is dense. You are glad to be
walking on a trail, for you have a link to civilization. The trail leads north
into a small town and south through the forest.
name: Hunting Trail

desc:
You stand in the town square of Tuckborough. Here, hobbits come out of their
burrows to meet, exchanging pleasantries, and sitting down for a smoke. Not far
from here is a large door, leading down into the ancestral home of the Tooks,
Great Smials.
name: Tuckborough Market Square

desc:
The scent of pipeweed is, quite obviously, very strong in here. Behind the
counter, Brenon Took smiles at you. He invites you to sample some of the new
brands, and it is very tempting to do so. There are bags and cases of pipeweed
in every corner, along with a small cabinet containing various pipes that are on
display. A sign hangs near the entrance.
name: Brenon Took's Shop of Pipes and Pipeweed

desc:
You walk in thick forest, over and around a large range of hills. There are
signs of life, all around you. While travel is pleasant, it is slow, and you
start to think you might get lost.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the south and east, the forest gets much
thicker.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the south and east, the forest gets much
thicker.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful. Westwards the
hill is too steep to climb.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
The hills are mostly regular, and the forest is dense. You are glad to be
walking on a trail. You can find your way to civilization, now. You notice there
are faint tracks of a cart leaving the trail here. The trail goes north and
west.
name: Hunting Trail

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You are standing in an ancient smial, apparently deserted. Broken furniture lies
strewn about the room, covered by a thin layer of soil. Strange. There are
footprints in the soil. Looking about, you see a rope on the western wall,
coiled about a spike. The footprints lead to this area, then stop.
name: Deserted Smial

desc:
This is a cold, damp cellar. An old, rickety ladder leads up, and a large grate
blocks your passage to the west. Light shines through a door in the south wall.
Wait... were those voices you just heard?
name: Cavern below the Deserted Smial

desc:
This is obviously a storage room. There are crates, sacks, and barrels all over
the room. However, some wooden barrels catch your attention, the gentle smell of
pipeweed wafting through the air. You stop to remember all those comfortable
evenings in front of the fire, listening to tales near and far. Why the heck are
you adventuring, anyways?
name: Pipeweed Storage

desc:
You have entered a small, square room. This room is kept in fine condition when
compared with the deserted room above. There is even a rug neatly covering the
floor. Some clothes hang on a rack, against the western wall, and some boots lie
underneath.
name: Pipeweed Smugglers' Hideout

desc:
The birch, maple, and pine all make an impression in these woods. Your foot
crunches upon the leaves and needles which make up the forest floor. Shafts of
sunlight break through the branches high above, and a soft breeze plays around
you. A quiet, gentle, woods you think, until you hear a dark howl to the south,
and then you wonder.
name: The Woodlands

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
After walking around this area for a while, you realise there's not much point
in heading further east. You'd only get lost.
name: Network of Corridors

desc:
Looking around, you realise you must be inside the Great Smials, home of the
Took family. Many passageways lead to other rooms. This place is large.
name: Inside Great Smials

desc:
This room is large, tall enough that noone needs to stoop to walk. You are in an
antechamber, with rooms north and south of you. Stairs go up. Several members of
the Took family have seen you, and stand up. These hobbits don't look so easily
cowed, though they offer you their hospitality.
name: Inside Great Smials

desc:
This appears to be a game room of sorts. Various playthings and boards are
scattered about, only a few of which you recognize.
name: Game Room

desc:
You have entered the office of the Thain, master of the Shire-moot, and captain
of the Shire-muster and the Hobbitry-in-arms. The office means less nowadays,
but it still holds significance. There are signs that someone here has been
drinking.
name: Thain's Office

desc:
There is a room to the south, and a long hallway leads north. Judging from the
clothes that one messy hobbit has left lying around, the living quarters are to
the north.
name: A Landing

desc:
Hmm... Perhaps that last step was a poor choice. You seem to have fallen into a
tangle of small traps and snares perfect for catching small game such as foxes
and rabbits. But since you are none of the above, perhaps some kind soul will
help you free, hopefully.
name: Plains

desc:
You look about in wonder. This plain appears to be well-tended. There are
various signs of cultivation. You realise that it is allowed to run wild, but in
a controlled state. The plain is teeming with life. You are standing in the
southwest corner of the plain. It stretches to the north and to the east.
name: A Well-tended Plain

desc:
This place is very narrow, lying beneath a foxes trap. A shallow pool of water
fills the bottom of the depression. The water, also a bit muddy, seems to be
safe.
name: Under the Trap

desc:
The road here continues through the heartland of the Shire. Small forests and
plains dot the horizon. Somewhere to the south-west is the village of Waymeet,
and farther past that Michel Delving. Eastwards lies the largest town in the
Shire, Hobbiton.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road makes a bend here in its long way. Rows of trees on the sides make your
walk comfortable as you go exploring the land known as the Shire. Forward to the
south-west is Waymeet. Beyond it is the sea, somewhere in the distance.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road continues, leading as always to mysterious places where an adventurer
can find a meaning to his life. Around you, the land seems to be very quiet and
peaceful. This is the nature of the hobbits. They just like to stay away from
troubles.
name: The East Road

desc:
The hills here have been a ploughed and excavated until the area is almost
level. A yoke and plough are lying in the northeast corner of the fields, and
there are a few cart and oxen tracks leading to the south. The grass is starting
to grow back in some areas and the fields look ready for planting. To the east,
you can see a small orchard.
name: Cultivated Landscape

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the south and east, the forest gets much
thicker.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
You start to walk down a dusty path through the plains. The plains to the west
look completely impassable. To the east you can enter the plains. Looking to
either side, you occasionally catch a glimpse of movement out of the corner of
your eyes. But you can never get a clear view of what it is.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You travel onwards along the East Road. The road runs east and west here. You
notice a path leading off to the south. You wonder where it goes.
name: The East Road

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You walk in a dense forest, up and around a seemingly infinite range of hills.
There are signs of life all around you. You relax somewhat, and the journey is
pleasant. However, you realise you could get lost, if not careful.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
The path seems to bend from the east to the south here. Looking south, you see
the path continue for a bit then disappear into the high grass. Glancing to the
west, you catch the sight of some movement at the edge of the road. Looking
back, you see nothing except the grass swaying in the wind.
name: A Dusty Path

desc:
You are well up on the tree. You see squirrels go whizzing by, playing an
intricate game of tag through the treetops. Looking out through the branches,
you marvel at the view. To the south-west you notice a small orchard. North-east
of here, you see a simple hobbit farm. To the south-east, you see a grassy
plain.
name: Up a Tree

desc:
Hanging on for dear life, you get buzzed by a couple of squirrels. You begin to
wonder what drove you to wander out on such a precarious perch. The view is
great but there are some prices too great to pay for just a view and your life
is certainly one of them.
name: Out on a Limb

desc:
Cold, rough walls of stone stand close about you on three sides. On the fourth
side there might as well be a wall, for the floor rises so sharpy that it will
take a good deal of luck or skill to make your way out of this dead end.
name: A Tunnel Going Down
